C-VV-HANDOFF-SKILL.IntentSummary: Implement the approved vv-handoff skill as a packaged vvoc managed skill. The outcome is a bundled SKILL.md that instructs agents to write project-local .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml notes from already-visible session context only, plus managed-skill registration, tests, README, and GRACE metadata updates proving install/sync delivery.

Baseline.Note: spec.xml is approved and has exactly one direct C-VV-HANDOFF-SKILL wrapper.
src/lib/managed-skills.ts defines MANAGED_SKILL_NAMES as vv-spec, vv-plan, vv-review, vv-execute, and vv-reflect.
templates/skills currently contains the five managed skill template directories and no vv-handoff directory.
installManagedSkillFiles and syncManagedSkillFiles already iterate MANAGED_SKILL_NAMES and copy optional references, so delivery should not require new install/sync control flow.
src/lib/opencode.test.ts managed-skill tests currently assert five SKILL.md files plus three reference files.
src/commands/init.test.ts currently asserts several managed skills are scaffolded during project init and must gain vv-handoff coverage.

Target.Note: templates/skills/vv-handoff/SKILL.md exists with YAML frontmatter name vv-handoff and clear behavioral contracts.
MANAGED_SKILL_NAMES includes vv-handoff so existing install/sync loops create and refresh it.
The vv-handoff skill writes only .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml, derives the slug itself from the main goal, and handles path collisions with -2, -3, and later suffixes.
The vv-handoff skill forbids running commands, tests, git status, git diff, or other fresh context collection and records unavailable evidence as not collected in current session.
The vv-handoff skill requires secret redaction to [REDACTED] before file writes.
README and GRACE metadata describe vv-handoff and the .vvoc/handoff artifact location.
Managed-skill tests cover vv-handoff distribution and core prompt contracts.

StaleStateWarnings.Warning: Implementation must read each target file fresh before editing; line numbers in this plan are context anchors only and may be stale.

If implementation discovers that OpenCode skill invocation requires controller trigger-rule changes for vv-handoff, stop and update the plan or get explicit confirmation before expanding scope to M-CLI-MANAGED-AGENTS.
Do not add command execution or validation behavior to vv-handoff even if it seems useful; the approved spec intentionally limits the skill to already-known session context.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Create templates/skills/vv-handoff/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter name vv-handoff and a concise description for end-of-session handoff note creation.

Skill instructions require using only current visible/known session context and explicitly forbid running shell commands, tests, lint, build, git status, git diff, web searches, or other fresh collection steps for the handoff.

Skill instructions require writing .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml, deriving session-slug from the main session goal, and using -2, -3, and later suffixes if the directory already exists.

Skill instructions define required XML sections for original request, completed work, current state and decisions, important or changed files, known checks/commands/results, blockers/risks/unknowns, and next safe step.
Skill instructions require replacing secrets such as tokens, API keys, passwords, cookies, private URLs, and similar sensitive values with [REDACTED] before writing.
Skill instructions state that missing git/diff/verification evidence must be written as not collected in current session rather than collected during handoff.
Skill instructions do not create or mention handoff.md as a canonical artifact and do not introduce schema validation.

T-002.Title: Register vv-handoff as a managed skill.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Add vv-handoff to MANAGED_SKILL_NAMES in src/lib/managed-skills.ts.
Update the MODULE_MAP and CHANGE_SUMMARY in src/lib/managed-skills.ts to mention vv-handoff.
Do not change installManagedSkillFiles or syncManagedSkillFiles unless tests prove the existing MANAGED_SKILL_NAMES loop is insufficient.

T-003.Title: Update managed-skill tests for distribution and prompt contracts.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Update src/lib/opencode.test.ts managed-skill count assertions from five managed SKILL.md files plus three references to six managed SKILL.md files plus three references.
Add assertions that installManagedSkillFiles, syncManagedSkillFiles, kept sync, and force sync include vv-handoff/SKILL.md.
Adjust skip-count expectations affected by adding one additional non-skipped skill.
Add a vv-handoff SKILL.md behavioral contract test covering the output path, no-command rule, required sections, not-collected wording, collision suffixing, and [REDACTED] secret handling.
Update src/commands/init.test.ts to assert project init scaffolds vv-handoff/SKILL.md.
Update test MODULE_MAP or CHANGE_SUMMARY comments where repository conventions require it.

T-004.Title: Update README and GRACE durable metadata.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Update README managed-skill overview from five to six workflow skills and add a vv-handoff table row.
Document .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml in the project-local data layout without implying validation or automatic command execution.
Update .grace/graph/main.xml M-CLI-MANAGED-SKILLS to include vv-handoff in the managed skill template purpose.
Update .grace/verification/main.xml V-M-CLI-MANAGED-SKILLS so the verification contract includes bun test src/lib/opencode.test.ts for managed-skill distribution behavior.
Update .grace/context/requirements.xml only to the extent needed to record the new managed handoff artifact location and skill purpose; do not duplicate the full spec.

T-005.Title: Run final focused and project-level verification.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Focused tests for managed skills and init pass.
Typecheck, lint, format check, and GRACE lint pass.
If any expected verification cannot be run, record the skipped command and reason before review or handoff.
